step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the selection of option B for hormone-sensitive lipase. Glucagon indeed regulates this enzyme, stimulating its release from fat cells to mobilize fatty acids during fasting states. Option A, lipoprotein lipase, is more involved in triglyceride breakdown in circulation rather than being directly controlled by glucagon. Therefore, my answer remains B.
